name: SLB Limited

profile.description: SLB (formerly Schlumberger) is the world's largest oilfield services company, providing technology, project management, and information solutions to the oil and gas industry globally. The company has pivoted to position itself as a global energy technology company with growing digital and data center businesses.

innovation.label: STRONG
innovation.analysis: Track Record (2/3): SLB rebranded from Schlumberger in 2022 and successfully pivoted to energy technology. The Digital division generates $2.4B annual run-rate with 32.7% margins and line of sight to 35%. Data Center Solutions revenue hit $331M in one quarter, up 140% YoY [2]. The ChampionX acquisition strengthens the production chemicals business. AI Deployment (2/2): SLB's Lumi AI platform and AI Factori service provide oilfield-specific generative AI. The Delfi platform is used by 85 of the top 100 global oil producers, leveraging AI to reduce drilling costs and improve recovery rates. Digital annual recurring revenue surpassed $1B [2]. Market Share (1/2): SLB is the world's largest oilfield services company by revenue, serving 85 of top 100 oil producers through Delfi. The company leads in digital oilfield solutions but faces competition from Halliburton and Baker Hughes [2]. Pipeline (1/2): Data center expansion targeting hyperscaler customers ('not driven by oil and gas customers' per CEO Le Peuch), representing a new growth vector beyond traditional oilfield services. However, 2026 revenue guidance of $36.9-37.7B is modest 3-5% growth [1]. R&D Intensity (1/1): SLB's R&D investment in AI, digital platforms, and new energy technologies exceeds oilfield services peers, supported by 109,000 employees globally [2].

inspiration.label: DEVELOPING
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (2/4): SLB's purpose — 'driving energy innovation for a balanced planet' — is specific and connects oilfield technology to the energy transition narrative. The 2022 rebrand was accompanied by a cultural evolution program ('Living our Culture') that attempted to make purpose operational. It's thoughtful but relatively new and still proving out [2]. Community Impact (1/2): SLB employs 109,000 globally and has sustainability commitments. The Diversity & Inclusion Director and Global Talent Manager roles show investment in inclusive culture. However, as an oilfield services company, the core business enables fossil fuel extraction, creating inherent tension with environmental community expectations [2]. Employee Retention (1/2): The 'Living our Culture' program focuses on active cultural embrace, and the company works to evolve from a historically siloed organization. CEO Le Peuch has been with the company his entire career, showing deep organizational commitment. Average retention data not available [2]. Insider Buying (1/2): Le Peuch has been CEO since 2019 and is a career SLB veteran, demonstrating long-term alignment. The board approved a 3.5% dividend increase and $4B+ shareholder returns for 2026, showing capital allocation confidence [1].

infrastructure.label: DEVELOPING
infrastructure.analysis: SLB's financial profile is moderate. D/E of 46.0% is conservative. ROE of 13.9% is close to but below the 15% target. Revenue declined 2% YoY with a 3-year CAGR of 8.3% [6]. Profit margin of 9.4% is well below the 20% target. Forward P/E of 15.2 is attractive. The dividend yield of 2.34% qualifies as a dividend stock [1][6]. Payout ratio of 48.5% is somewhat elevated but manageable given strong free cash flow of $4.11B. Consecutive years paid: 44, which is excellent. Consecutive years growth: 4, which is modest as the company cut its dividend during the 2020 downturn. 5-year dividend growth CAGR of 5.4% is decent but below the 6% target [1]. Liquidity floor easily cleared: 20.3M × $50.51 = $1.03B daily [6].

ai_resilience.label: LOW RISK
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (8/9): SLB's core oilfield services business — drilling, completion, production optimization, and reservoir management — involves physical-world operations with massive infrastructure that AI cannot substitute. The company's 109,000 employees operate equipment, manage wells, and provide on-site technical services that require physical presence. AI is purely an enabler (Delfi, Lumi) that enhances service delivery rather than threatening it. Furthermore, SLB is actively expanding into AI data center infrastructure, positioning as a beneficiary of the AI buildout [2]. AI Opportunity (1/1): SLB generates meaningful AI revenue — Digital annual recurring revenue surpassed $1B, the Lumi AI platform and Delfi serve 85 of the top 100 oil producers, and Data Center Solutions revenue hit $331M quarterly (up 140% YoY). CEO Le Peuch explicitly stated data center revenue is driven by 'hyperscaler partners responding to the AI boom' [2].

infinite_game.summary: SLB demonstrates genuine infinite game qualities through its century-long heritage, purpose-driven rebrand, and transformative digital pivot. The 'Living our Culture' program and data center expansion show forward thinking, but the 2020 dividend cut and commodity cycle vulnerability reveal finite pressures.

just_cause.analysis: SLB's cause — driving energy innovation for a balanced planet — was articulated during the 2022 rebrand and served as 'the filter for every major investment and strategic decision.' While this purpose is more aspirational than proven over decades, the 100+ year heritage of the original Schlumberger provides institutional memory of endurance. The data center pivot shows willingness to evolve beyond oil and gas [2]. (1/3 pts)

trusting_teams.analysis: The 'Living our Culture' program, Diversity & Inclusion Director, and Global Talent Manager roles demonstrate investment in organizational trust. CEO Le Peuch is a career veteran who has led cultural transformation. The governance ratings are among the highest in the energy sector [2]. However, the 2020 dividend cut during COVID and layoffs show the company will sacrifice employee interests when pressured. (1/2 pts)

worthy_rivals.analysis: SLB competes constructively with Halliburton and Baker Hughes, using competitive pressure to drive its digital transformation rather than engaging in zero-sum market share battles. The company's pivot to energy technology and data centers shows learning from tech industry rivals rather than dismissing them [2]. (1/1 pt)

existential_flexibility.analysis: The pivot from pure oilfield services to energy technology company — including the data center expansion serving hyperscaler customers — represents significant strategic flexibility. The rebrand from Schlumberger to SLB signals willingness to evolve identity. However, the core oilfield services business remains dominant, making this evolution rather than transformation [1][2]. (1/2 pts)

courage_to_lead.analysis: The $1B+ digital ARR and data center expansion demonstrate courageous long-term investment in non-traditional business lines. The ChampionX acquisition during uncertain oil markets shows capital allocation conviction. However, the 2020 dividend cut (first in decades) and conservative 2026 guidance suggest management prioritizes near-term stability over bold bets [1][2]. (1/2 pts)

investment_thesis: SLB is the world's leading energy technology company trading at a forward P/E of 15.2x with a 2.34% dividend yield and $4B+ committed shareholder returns for 2026 [1]. The Digital division ($1B+ ARR at 32.7% margins) and Data Center Solutions ($331M quarterly revenue, 140% growth) represent a high-value transformation from cyclical oilfield services to recurring, technology-driven revenue. AI platforms Lumi and Delfi serve 85 of the top 100 global oil producers, creating deep customer relationships with high switching costs. The 3.5% dividend increase signals management confidence in the sustainability of cash flows [1][2].
thesis_continued: The bear case centers on commodity cycle exposure: despite the digital pivot, the majority of SLB's $35.7B revenue remains tied to upstream oil and gas spending, which declined 2% in 2025. EPS fell 24% YoY (excluding charges) on lower commodity prices and reduced client spending [1]. The company guided for a further Q1 2026 revenue dip with 6-9 cent EPS headwinds. The dividend was cut in 2020 and only restored 4 years of consecutive growth, making the income stream less reliable than energy majors like XOM. Profit margins of 9.4% are thin for a technology-positioning company, and the data center expansion faces intense competition from pure-play providers [1][2].
key_catalysts: Digital annual recurring revenue exceeding $1B with path to 35% margins, transforming revenue quality
Data Center Solutions revenue growing 140% YoY as hyperscalers leverage SLB's energy infrastructure expertise
$4B+ shareholder returns in 2026 including 3.5% dividend increase to $0.295/quarter
key_risks: Core oilfield services revenue still commodity-cycle dependent — 2025 revenue declined 2% on lower upstream spending
Q1 2026 expected revenue dip and 6-9 cent EPS headwinds signal near-term softness in traditional business
Thin 9.4% profit margins provide limited buffer against sustained commodity price weakness or macro downturn
sources: Entrepreneur — Oilfield Leader SLB: An AI Name You Need to Know, 2024
Oil & Gas IQ — Understanding SLB's Culture Evolution, Innovation and Leadership, 2024
AINvest — Schlumberger Surges on Strong Earnings and Digital Transformation, March 2026
Yahoo Finance — SLB key statistics, accessed March 2026
